steps.title: Identify the interior angles of the triangle
steps.content: The figure shows a triangle with three interior angles labeled. At the bottom-left vertex (where the triangle meets line m), the interior angle is $y° = 20°$. At the right, where the triangle meets line l, the interior angle is $z° = 60°$. At the top vertex, the interior angle is $x°$ (what we're solving for).

steps.title: Apply the triangle angle sum theorem
steps.content:
The sum of the three interior angles in any triangle must equal \$180°$. Set up the equation:

$$x + y + z = 180°$$

Substitute the known values:

$$x + 20 + 60 = 180$$

$$x + 80 = 180$$

$$x = 100$$

That matches choice B — B is correct.
